items.title: IDC study highlights identity resilience gaps as AI adoption accelerates
items.text: Many organizations face identity management gaps for AI systems. Most businesses experienced cyber incidents, highlighting security vulnerabilities. Improved collaboration between IT and security teams is now essential. Organizations are also struggling with cyber resilience and recovery planning. A new assessment tool helps evaluate cyber readiness across key areas.

items.title: Why CISOs in India should rethink identity security in the age of artificial intelligence
items.text: Indian CISOs are prioritizing identity security as AI adoption accelerates across industries. Many organizations face delays in AI initiatives due to identity-related security friction. Reducing unnecessary access and replacing permanent privileges with temporary access are key actions. Credential security must be treated as a leadership priority, not just technical maintenance. Building resilience through stronger identity controls protects businesses from AI-driven exploitation.

items.title: TRAI likely to tweak regulation for spam data sharing by call management apps soon
items.text: TRAI plans to amend regulations for commercial communications soon. Call management apps may share user-reported spam details with telecom operators. This aims to strengthen the fight against unwanted calls and messages. The regulator is examining stakeholder feedback on the proposed changes.

items.title: AI is reshaping cybersecurity - for better and worse
items.text: Artificial intelligence is transforming cybersecurity for both defenders and attackers. Machine learning models improve detection and response capabilities significantly. However, threat actors also leverage AI for more sophisticated phishing campaigns. Organizations must now focus on responsible AI deployment and robust governance. This evolving landscape necessitates continuous adaptation and human oversight for cyber resilience.

items.title: India must build resilient software and hardware systems to withstand cyber threats
items.text: India must build strong and reliable software and hardware systems that can keep working even during cyberattacks. This resilience is important to support daily life and make it easier to do business as the country becomes more digital, said S. Krishnan, Secretary of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), on Monday.

items.title: India considers stronger safeguards as online fraud surges
items.text: India's central bank is seeking recommendations to tackle a surge in online fraud as scammers siphon off billions of dollars annually from users of its widely used digital payments network. Users can send the bank recommendations until May 9.

items.title: Why Zero Trust is becoming non-negotiable
items.text: Cloud transformation necessitates Zero Trust for modern enterprise security. Traditional perimeters are collapsing, making identity the new attack surface. AI adoption and multi-cloud complexity increase security challenges significantly. Zero Trust ensures continuous verification for all users and systems. This principle is vital for business resilience and responsible AI integration.

items.title: Beyond the blind trust: The hard questions security leaders must ask about AI
items.text: Enterprise technology faces risks with rapid AI adoption and slow integration. CISOs must enable safe AI implementation by addressing people, process, and technology. AI literacy and developer awareness are crucial for mitigating human-driven errors. Robust processes and least privilege principles build essential AI guardrails. Technology provides control and visibility, ensuring explainability and cost management.

items.title: Digital trust in 2026: Why privacy must be paired with accountability
items.text: Digital platforms grapple with balancing user privacy and preventing cybercrime. Concerns arise over platforms enabling child abuse and drug trafficking channels. Cybercriminals exploit communication tools for credential theft and illicit activity coordination. Platforms must develop safeguards against misuse while protecting valid privacy rights. Achieving this balance is crucial for trusted digital ecosystems moving forward.

items.title: The expanding API attack surface in the age of AI
items.text: Application programming interfaces are becoming a significant attack surface due to AI's rapid expansion. AI agents are now accelerating these security risks, making defense more challenging. Security leaders must establish complete visibility and categorize APIs based on business risk. Conducting threat modeling and continuously managing API security posture are essential steps. Implementing layered protection controls and strengthening access authorization will mitigate API attacks.

items.title: Critical SharePoint RCE flaw exploited to steal machine keys
items.text: Hackers are actively exploiting the critical CVE-2026-50522 vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint to steal machine keys and maintain access even after affected servers are patched. [...]

items.title: Anubis ransomware claims Coca-Cola Fairlife attack, threatens data leak
items.text: The Anubis ransomware gang has claimed responsibility for the cyberattack on Coca-Cola's Fairlife dairy subsidiary, threatening to publish allegedly stolen corporate data unless the company pays a ransom. [...]

items.title: Apple Fixes Hide My Email Bug That Exposed Real Addresses in Mail Logs
items.text: Apple has moved to address a security flaw in its Hide My Email service that enabled users' real email addresses to be unmasked, effectively undermining the feature's privacy guarantees. 404 Media reported Tuesday that a fix for the issue was deployed by Apple on July 3, 2026, after more than a year, when it was disclosed to the company by Tyler Murphy, co-founder of EasyOptOuts. Hide My Email

items.title: Critical wp2shell WordPress flaws exploited to install webshells
items.text: Hackers are exploiting the "wp2shell" critical vulnerability suite (CVE-2026-63030 and CVE-2026-60137) affecting WordPress Core to deploy persistent webshells and install malicious plugins on affected servers. [...]

items.title: AWS Kiro Flaw Let a Poisoned Web Page Rewrite Its Config and Run Code
items.text: Hidden text on a web page was enough to make Kiro, AWS's agentic coding IDE, rewrite its own configuration file and run an attacker's code on a developer's machine, with no approval step able to stop it. Intezer, in research with Kodem Security, found that a request as ordinary as asking Kiro to summarize a page could end in remote code execution. AWS has patched the issue, and no CVE has been

items.title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber AI to Find and Fix Software Vulnerabilities
items.text: Google's DeepMind on Tuesday announced the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, a specialized artificial intelligence (AI) model built atop 3.5 Flash that's designed to discover, validate, and patch vulnerabilities quickly and efficiently. According to the tech giant, the model will be exclusively available to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender as part of a limited-access pilot

items.title: Critical SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-50522 Under Active Exploitation After Public PoC
items.text: A third SharePoint Server flaw patched by Microsoft as part of its Patch Tuesday update for July 2026 has come under active exploitation, per watchTowr. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-50522 (CVSS score: 9.8), a critical deserialization of untrusted data in Microsoft Office SharePoint that could allow an unauthorized attacker to execute code over a network. Microsoft credited DEVCORE

items.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
items.text: Threat actors have been observed exploiting a now-patched high-severity Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS vulnerability as an entry point to deploy Qilin (aka Agenda) ransomware on victim environments. Arctic Wolf Labs said it investigated multiple intrusions in June 2026 that began with the exploitation of CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), an authentication bypass flaw affecting the portal and gateway

items.title: Closing the Identity Gaps in Critical Infrastructure Security
items.text: Critical infrastructure attacks often begin with stolen credentials, compromised devices, or trusted accounts. Specops Software explains why Zero Trust should verify both user identities and device trust before granting access to critical systems. [...]

items.title: Zimbra Patches Critical SNMP Command Injection and Four XSS Vulnerabilities
items.text: Zimbra has rolled out fixes to address multiple critical security issues, including a command injection flaw in the Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) monitoring component. As many as nine security vulnerabilities have been patched in Zimbra 10.1.20. Topping the list is a command injection vulnerability in the SNMP monitoring component when SNMP notifications are enabled. Also patched

items.text: View CSAF Summary SIDIS Secured SmartPlug before V7.26.0310 is affected by multiple vulnerabilities in the components OpenSSL, OpenSSH, and several other packages as described below. Siemens has released a new version of SIDIS Secured SmartPlug and recommends to update to the latest version. items.title: CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
items.text: CISA has added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2021-27137 DD-WRT Stack-Based Buffer Overflow Vulnerability CVE-2026-0770 Langflow Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere Vulnerability   CVE-2026-63030 WordPress Core Interpretation Conflict Vulnerability   CVE-2026-60137 WordPress Core SQL Injection Vulnerability These types of vulnerabilities are frequent attack vectors for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise. Binding Operational Direc

items.title: Siemens IAM Client
items.text: View CSAF Summary Multiple Siemens products are affected by unquoted search path vulnerability in IAM Client. This could allow an authenticated local attacker to perform privilege escalation. Siemens has released new versions for several affected products and recommends to update to the latest versions. Siemens is preparing further fix versions and recommends countermeasures for products where fixes are not, or not yet available. The following versions of Siemens IAM Client are affected: COMOS V10.4.5 vers:intdot/<10.4.5.0.2  COMOS V10.6 vers:intdot/<10.6.1  Designcenter NX ver

Customers are advised to consult and implement the workarounds provided in Palo Alto Networks' upstream security notifications. items.title: Open-Source Android AI Agents Could Let Invisible Screen Text Run Code on Host PCs
items.text: An Android app that can draw over other windows and write to shared storage can slip instructions to the AI agent driving that phone, in text no human eye will ever see. Two more steps, and the same app is running commands on the PC driving the agent. Researchers demonstrated that chain, plus six other attacks, against five open-source mobile agent frameworks: AppAgent, AppAgentX,

items.title: N-day is Becoming N-Hour. Patching Faster Won't Save You.
items.text: Every patch is a confession. The moment a vendor ships a security fix, the diff between the old code and the new code tells anyone watching exactly what was broken and where. Turn that diff back into a working exploit, and you can hit every system that hasn't updated yet. This is N-day exploitation, and it's always been a race: the vendor patches, the clock starts, and defenders try to deploy

items.title: New Bit2Watt Attack Could Let Cloud Tenants Disrupt Power Grids Without an Exploit
items.text: A cloud tenant using nothing but ordinary GPU access can push a data center's power draw up and down fast enough to threaten the grid it runs on, with no exploit and no break-in. That is the claim behind Bit2Watt, described by three Zhejiang University researchers in a paper accepted to CHES 2026, the IACR's hardware-security conference, and the evidence splits in two: they measured the power

items.title: US seizes over 1,000 websites in FIFA World Cup piracy crackdown
items.text: The U.S. Justice Department has seized more than 1,000 websites and blocked 1,970 domains used to stream FIFA World Cup 2026 matches without authorization. [...]

items.title: Critical Palo Alto VPN bug now exploited by Qilin ransomware gang
items.text: The Qilin ransomware gang is exploiting a critical PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw to breach victims' networks, according to cybersecurity company Arctic Wolf. [...]

items.title: Microsoft shares manual fix for WSUS sync delays and timeouts
items.text: Microsoft has shared manual mitigations to help IT administrators fix Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) servers affected by a known issue that causes Windows Update scans to fail or time out. [...]

items.title: WordPress wp2shell Exploitation Grows as Public Exploit Fuels Mass Scanning
items.text: Attackers have begun to exploit two critical vulnerabilities in WordPress that, when combined together, enable unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) and complete compromise of vulnerable websites. The two security flaws, tracked as CVE-2026-63030 and CVE-2026-60137, have been codenamed wp2shell. "By the early hours of Saturday morning (UTC), successful exploitation was already well

items.title: Windows LegacyHive zero-day flaw gets free, unofficial patches
items.text: Free unofficial patches are available for a recently disclosed Windows zero-day flaw that allows attackers to escalate privileges on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]

items.title: Estée Lauder discloses data breach via Oracle E-Business flaw
items.text: Cosmetics giant Estée Lauder is notifying employees of a data breach after hackers exploited a flaw in Oracle E-Business Suite that the company used for human resources (HR) operations. [...]

items.title: SonicWall SMA1000 flaws exploited as zero-days to push custom malware
items.text: Two recently disclosed SonicWall SMA1000 vulnerabilities were exploited in zero-day attacks for weeks, allowing threat actors to install custom malware on vulnerable VPN appliances. [...]

note: India-first CTI from free, purpose-built sources (ET CISO India, CISA KEV/advisories, security news RSS; optional OTX/ThreatFox with free keys). CERT-In has no working feed. No social-media scraping. Intel is enrichment — alerts are simulated and human-gated.
